---
title: 'Mike (Michael) C Wolfe (05/1954 - 01/03/2025)'
date: '2017-12-04T09:05:00.0180000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Wolfe Family Tree'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1153363584477100975'
blogger_filename: '/2017/12/mike-michael-c-wolfe-xxxx-xxxx.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Wolfe Family Tree/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Mike Wolfe'
---

<h3 _msthash="1281" _msttexthash="1479855" style="text-align: left;">Michael Craig Wolfe (05/14/1954 - 01/03/2025) - Mike Wolfe</h3><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEiBh2QZONZiOYTN7J21VgZ8pKbC0rqy-bvt8LhcaBGNN3_S2e4iQeBzNuVM63rvWQGZtOYklR7YsBuUkQv9SG9Jn0nEtQcHyGmQ6pKB0mVwZNtz2d6E-qGNtygDHh6pZz9__i4_RYH6eIAuBlTkq8SB7-Zd9UySzgUettccNqwflSlJ7rz3IwzNXjSqCmCm/s2048/Messenger_creation_D34FEDB2-504D-40B6-B1BF-7CA2C50D65EC.heic" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" data-original-height="2048" data-original-width="1536" height="320" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEiBh2QZONZiOYTN7J21VgZ8pKbC0rqy-bvt8LhcaBGNN3_S2e4iQeBzNuVM63rvWQGZtOYklR7YsBuUkQv9SG9Jn0nEtQcHyGmQ6pKB0mVwZNtz2d6E-qGNtygDHh6pZz9__i4_RYH6eIAuBlTkq8SB7-Zd9UySzgUettccNqwflSlJ7rz3IwzNXjSqCmCm/s320/Messenger_creation_D34FEDB2-504D-40B6-B1BF-7CA2C50D65EC.heic" width="240" /></a></td></tr><tr><td _msthash="1282" _msttexthash="352235" class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">Christmas Day 12/24/2024</td></tr></tbody></table><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhApVAypm9pQ7n0FIt2VN3tRPzJQkV5tQbAXFJKAqmgVfrPgSHuypHxVdwsDhKekX5cL8Pjsksh4hBjyMPoumoUC0rRXKk1Qxx8FKi55hekqe6X3kDA7XcsKyZEJYa1bL5yYtZq57cPtY-8MnsOvHNQavvinS3gxqs9ozwIHpQdmWphJTwFE88GxKv4vZVq/s4032/IMG_20250103_170135.heic" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" data-original-height="4032" data-original-width="3024" height="320" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhApVAypm9pQ7n0FIt2VN3tRPzJQkV5tQbAXFJKAqmgVfrPgSHuypHxVdwsDhKekX5cL8Pjsksh4hBjyMPoumoUC0rRXKk1Qxx8FKi55hekqe6X3kDA7XcsKyZEJYa1bL5yYtZq57cPtY-8MnsOvHNQavvinS3gxqs9ozwIHpQdmWphJTwFE88GxKv4vZVq/s320/IMG_20250103_170135.heic" width="240" /></a></td></tr><tr><td _msthash="1283" _msttexthash="267098" class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">Morning of 01/03/2025</td></tr></tbody></table><br /><p><i _msthash="1284" _msttexthash="36145681">*This page is written by Mike Wolfe's son, Darrell Wolfe, with input from those who shared stories. I will add to this page periodically, if and when new stories are shared. The following is a photo album of his life such as we have them digitized. <a href="https://photos.app.goo.gl/CudcZfxERiowJv8H7" target="_blank">Mike Wolfe Google Photos</a>. I won't sugar coat it, he was complicated.</i></p><p><br /></p><h3 _msthash="1285" _msttexthash="4468906" style="text-align: left;">This is the story of Michael C Wolfe, as told by his eldest son, with additions from others.</h3><p _msthash="1286" _msttexthash="2639247">Mike was born May 14, 1954 (in?) to Donald F Wolfe and Maryan Louise King&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1287" _msttexthash="16885869">He lived a complicated life, and those who knew him may have different ways of describing his impact. As his son, I would describe his life as "wasted potential", but not entirely "bad".&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1288" _msttexthash="11162723">My understanding of his early years is that he lived in a trailer park near Baldwin Park with his father Donald F Wolfe and mother Maryan Wolfe (King).&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1289" _msttexthash="60210293">At 13-years old, Mike woke up to his father Donald beating his mother Maryan&nbsp;in the head with a monkey wrench, and he pulled a baseball bat from under his bed to knock his father off of his mother. Donald went to jail for a number of years, and Maryanne had at least 113 stitches in her head. In many ways, Mike never grew emotionally passed that of a 13-year old.&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1290" _msttexthash="34431319">In his teen years, Mike became the captain of the drumline for his high school marching band. He got a job as a cook at a breakfast place (Denny's, or iHop, or something like it) to earn money for a motorcycle. He got his motorcycle license at 16 and bought a bike for cash.&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1291" _msttexthash="44006274">He had been in early college finishing his general education credits planning to go into dentistry when he came into contact with "Christian Apologetics" and he decided that Christianity was the most reasonable explanation for the way of things. He changed his career plans to go into ministry.</p><p _msthash="1292" _msttexthash="4791059">He was a youth pastor at 1st Christian Church of Alhambra California when he met Debbie Tweedy.</p><p _msthash="1293" _msttexthash="7757074">Mike Wolfe married Debbie Tweedy April 7, 1979 (Records say 04/08 due to a clerical error by the pastor). They had three sons:&nbsp;</p><p></p><ol style="text-align: left;"><li _msthash="1294" _msttexthash="269880">Darrell Wolfe - 1980</li><li _msthash="1295" _msttexthash="216736">Devin Wolfe - 1983</li><li _msthash="1296" _msttexthash="193882">Ryan Wolfe - 1988</li></ol><div _msthash="1297" _msttexthash="11670048">Mike graduated with his Masters of Divinity (MDiv) and Doctorate of Ministry (DMin) from Claremont Theological Seminary simultaneously on May 1, 1983.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1298" _msttexthash="28109835">I once thought he was inspired to do his doctoral dissertation on Church Growth because of Bill Hybels. He later corrected me on this point. He said he was not aware of Bill Hybels until after he was a senior pastor at Agoura Hills around 1986.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/screenshot-20250104-135022-f215121188ac.png" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="1698" data-original-width="1080" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/screenshot-20250104-135022-cb4bafff2cc1.png" width="204" /></a></div><br /><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1299" _msttexthash="26879411">He pastored within the Disciples of Christ (DOC), also known as (Aka) "1st Christian Church Of... (insert city here)" from the late 70s through 1996. He was senior pastor in Bellflower CA, Agoura Hills CA, Spearman TX, Indio CA, and Norwalk CA.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1300" _msttexthash="133386110">Throughout his ministry, he was an alcoholic pastor. He once told me a story, in which my mom (Debbie) was working overnight shifts as a nurse and he left me alone at six-months old in a crib so he could go so the store to get more alcohol. He was pulled over by a cop but he wasn't noticed to be drunk because he hid is well, and he was let go. I often wonder if he had been arrested for a DUI at that point, in 1980, his alcoholism would have been public early and everyone (including him) could have had the opportunity to address it earlier.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1301" _msttexthash="137415642">Throughout his seminary he became increasingly concerned with idiosyncrasies between the common mythology of modern western Christianity and the academic realities of the Bible. Mike was most profoundly affected by the work of&nbsp;<a href="https://virtualreligion.net/primer/bultmann.html" target="_blank">Rudolf Karl Bultmann</a> around the idea of Demythologizing Jesus. This led Mike on a journey away from orthodox evangelical Christianity toward atheism. As his personal beliefs became more liberal/unorthodox, he told me that he made sure his preaching became more conservative to hide that fact.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1302" _msttexthash="118261403">It was during his second to last church posting, Coachella Valley Christian Church, Indio CA that his "journey to the dark side" was complete. He realized he couldn't continue a career as a pastor in which he had no faith, so he began working on a second masters degree, this time in Marriage and Family Therapy (MFT), ironic since he was so very bad at both marriage an family to the very end. Then again, don't we all specialize in our weaknesses? As the saying goes, "There, but for the grace of God, go I..."</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1303" _msttexthash="82364321">I remember it was during this church season (1989-1994) that I was homeschooled for a bit, I used to do schoolwork during the week in the empty classrooms and then play GI Joes in the sanctuary. He used to give us cash and we three boys would go across the street to the market to get snacks and nachos. This was the last season I can recall feeling like we had something akin to a family, albeit a dysfunctional family.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1304" _msttexthash="13884507">At some point close to 1992-1994, my mom (Debbie) learned of his alcoholism and atheism, and had a brief breakdown, grieving the loss of what she thought she married.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1305" _msttexthash="98972575">Mike tried to stay connected with his Mother Maryan and sister Linda (and her family), but when his addiction became known, they cut him off fairly completely to avoid being victims of his badness. Which is an overreaction born of their religious hypocrisy, but an understandable reaction for people with no concept of mental health or healthy boundaries. It's all or nothing for the deeply religious people who had religion but clearly no Jesus.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1306" _msttexthash="36891790">It really worked for us (our side of the family), because Linda was a twat-waffle who deserved every bad thing that could ever happen to her... but Maryan was kind of decent to us most of the time. I (Darrell) and my wife and son remained connected to Maryan until her death in 2012.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1307" _msttexthash="470637063">RE: Donald Wolfe -- The date of this event is unclear, but Mike made attempts to connect with Donald Wolfe over the years, sending him Christmas presents and photos of the family. One year, we went to visit Donald at the apartment he was living in and renovating. I am told that Donald went to the closet and pulled out a box of all the presents we'd sent over the years, saying, "I don't' really deserve these..." and handed Mike the box of presents unopened. While from Don's perspective, this makes sense. He didn't deserve them. He was never a real father to Mike, it is also easy to see the rejection that this created in Mike for his father to reject his bids for connection. This was a fairly pivotal moment, I think, for his adult attempts at recovery from his own mental illness. Donald G Wolfe was found among homeless in an abandoned building, an alcoholic, died from Alcoholism and Emphysema, and in a puddle of his own filth. Ironically this would mirror the Mike's final passing in several ways.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1308" _msttexthash="46277374">In 1994, Mike moved us from the Indio church to the First Christian Church of Norwalk, CA, the only church he got hired at while actively being a secret atheist. He pastored this final church from 1994-1996. At least two men were profoundly impacted by Mike during these two years. They provided the following:&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><i></i></div><blockquote><div><i _msthash="1309" _msttexthash="52313430">This is from Dewie J from the Norwalk Church: Neil M was one of the amazing elders at Bellflower, he and his wife loved you as a baby. Neil has prayed over Mike for years. They both reached out a few weeks ago and weâ€™re trying to find him and go see him. Next from Dewey:</i></div><div><div><i><br /></i></div><div><i _msthash="1310" _msttexthash="1303380">I got an email and talked to Neil. I'm sorry too!&nbsp;</i></div><div><i><br /></i></div><div><i _msthash="1311" _msttexthash="4172324">It breaks my heart that he helped me so much and there was nothing that I could do for him.&nbsp;</i></div><div><i><br /></i></div><div><i _msthash="1312" _msttexthash="14667419">I'm so grateful that I met you and Mike and your family. It was one of the highlights of my life. I honor you and your family by stating clean and sober and serving at church.</i></div><div><i><br /></i></div><div><i _msthash="1313" _msttexthash="13201214">Working with Mike in my beginnings of sobriety was the foundation I needed and I never stopped there. June 24th, I will celebrate 31 years sober. To God be the glory!</i></div><div><i><br /></i></div><div><i _msthash="1314" _msttexthash="3759600">Let me know if you ever get to Bellflower. Neil and I would love to catch up with you.</i></div></div><div></div></blockquote><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1315" _msttexthash="10362118">Mike stayed at the Norwalk church two years while he finished his degree in Fullerton, CA. After which, he left the ministry for the last time.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><i _msthash="1316" _msttexthash="14690416">*That is with one caveat, he wrote about being an atheist pastor, and he spent some time preaching about being a spiritual atheist in Unitarian Universalist churches.</i></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1317" _msttexthash="88930309">It was after this period that the entire family settled back into our old home in Indio, CA, which had been rented out while we were in Orange County, CA. We spent 1996-1999 in a sort of status quo. At some point in there, he took each of us boys out to tell us about his journey and that he was now an atheist. By this point, we had such little respect for him, that we didn't care much, to be honest. I probably cared more than Devin or Ryan did.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1318" _msttexthash="12159485">There were moments, brief moments, that I can recall him being fatherly? According to a lot of photo evidence, he was at least around more than I remember.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1319" _msttexthash="25726337">It was during this season that I (Darrell) and mom (Debbie) started attending Al-Anon meetings and learning about boundaries and codependency and other addiction family dynamics (such as we understood them in the late 1990s).&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1320" _msttexthash="121141709">During this period, his alcoholism progressed into barbiturate abuse (stealing Debbie's migraine medications). Mike agreed to attend Narcotics Anonymous (NA) for this new addiction, and according to his account to me (Darrell), while he was at the NA meeting he heard two young men say, "F- this, lets go get high" and he thought to himself, "You got to try anything once." and he went with them. This is when he picked up a new habit that would largely define his life from that day forward, Crack Cocaine.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1321" _msttexthash="70072717">Very shortly after this fateful event, in 1999, I (Darrell, age 19), came home one day to Debbie (my mom) distraught, "Mike is in the garage smoking crack!". I went into the garage and told him, "You've forfeited your rights to this house, leave in the next 20 minutes or you'll leave in an ambulance." He calmly said, "I understand." and about ten minute later, his car started and he left.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1322" _msttexthash="2918110">He spent time on the streets, he spent time in and out of halfway houses.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1323" _msttexthash="1345214">Mike and Debbie finalized their divorce in 2000.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1324" _msttexthash="11852477">Throughout the early 2000s, Mike returned to the house to steal valuables and sell them for drugs. He stole and sold fine China, silver, and other items.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1325" _msttexthash="13140894">I wasn't present for this event, but according to my call with Ryan just now (01/04/2025), who provided better details on the story I heard third hand previously:&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1326" _msttexthash="63087557">One day, Mike returned to steal more stuff and this is when Ryan and Devin found out he'd stolen the China that Debbie had inherited from her Aunt Blanche and Uncle Glen. They wanted to beat him up with golf clubs but couldn't find any, so they beat him with pool cues until he finally left. Given Mike's 13-year old story, this was an off circle of life moment, I think.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1327" _msttexthash="46065032">Throughout the remaining 2000's Mike was in and out of halfway houses, he would get sober, explain his resume gap through some kind of "Sabbatical" excuse, and then get hired as a counselor/therapist somewhere. Roughly every few years, he would go radio silent, and we'd find out he was on the street again.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1328" _msttexthash="61903192">On one occasion, fairly early on, pre-2004, Mike called our house phone in a disguised voice pretending to be a detective pronouncing "Mike Wolfe" dead, but he couldn't hold his tone straight and we eventually figured it was him. For some reason, he thought this prank was hilarious. For us, it was confirmation that his mental illness was getting worse.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1329" _msttexthash="24134877">At some point, he sort of stabilized, ish? At least, from all outward appearances, he was still acting&nbsp; more and more bizarre, his humor devolving to that of a 12-13 year old boy. But he kept a job and even made friends at work.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1330" _msttexthash="51832950">In July 2009, I (Darrell) moved with my family to Texas, where we stayed until 2017, when we moved to Idaho. In all our years in Texas, we invited Mike many times. He would often reply with the same decline, "The grand kids don't need my presence, just my money" and he would politely decline the invitation and send money instead.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1331" _msttexthash="28085915">On October 20, 2009, his son Devin died of a complication due to drugs and alcohol. Mike expressed deep grief over this event and dotted on Devin's daughter Kaylee (within the limitations of his own mental illness) from that day forward.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1332" _msttexthash="6752057">At his final job, he met Marielena. They didn't date while co-workers, but when he left the job, they began dating.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1333" _msttexthash="1405209">On 10/16/2010 Mike began dating Marielena Rubio.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1334" _msttexthash="18855954">His last job as a therapist was with <a href="https://locator.lacounty.gov/lac/Location/3176806/challenger-memorial-youth-center" target="_blank">Los Angeles County Juvenile Justice</a> camps, which "Provides Secure Detention For Delinquent Minors In Juvenile Halls And Rehabilitation Programs In Camps."</div><div><div><br /></div></div><div _msthash="1335" _msttexthash="18990231">Due more to the way he treated his diet and failed to care for his body and to his addictions, than to any genetic component, he developed such severe back issues that he was in near constant pain.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1336" _msttexthash="18198934">There are two things Mike Wolfe cannot abide, it is pain and boredom. He once told me that boredom was worse that death, and physically painful for him. He had to have his mind distracted 24-7-365.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1337" _msttexthash="39794027">As a result of years of counseling and learning about neurodivergence, I recognize this as dopamine deficiency. Given that everyone in our family was ADHD, Autistic, or both, it's highly likely he was Autistic and this was a dopamine issue. But there is no way to be sure at this point.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1338" _msttexthash="54921061">In Fall 2016, Mike ended up retiring 8.5 years into his county career. As it takes 10 to be vested, he did not receive any pension or retirement from the county. Further, the money he had saved he spent all at once in a manic insistence on taking a cruise to Hawaii that year, regardless of who could move their schedules to make the invite.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1339" _msttexthash="18385120">He claimed he retired to his "Preferred Purposes Status", but this was largely self-delusional. He had no purpose, no direction, and he spiraled in his mental and physical health to the end.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1340" _msttexthash="7743593">Between 2016-2019, he had his back fused and tried to find a way to live off of Social Security and government assistance.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1341" _msttexthash="139847552">In 2014, I (Darrell), my wife (Flavia) and our two boys (Connor and Preston), went to California to visit everyone (Mom/Debbie paid for the trip). Along with Devin's daughter, Kaylee, we all went to Disneyland, including Debbie and Mike's girlfriend Marielena. Mike was into "the experience" so he paid for us to eat inside the cafe of Pirates of the Caribbean, and my oldest Connor remembers doing the&nbsp;Light Saber experience, where a Master Jedi helps you build your own light saber. Preston has no memory of the trip, but Connor still does.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1342" _msttexthash="384096921">On June 25, 2018, My (Darrell) wife (Flavia) died, and Mike took that hard too. He loved her, and she treated him really well. Despite my complicated history with him, she respected his position as the boys grandfather and always included him in Christmas and birthday celebrations (whether he sent money or gifts or not), and made sure the kids would make him some small hand drawn card or something to send him. She ensured he got to see his grand kids. After her passing, I still tried to include him, but neither of us tried as hard as she died, and the boys really know him much today. They haven't talked with him on the phone in years, and weren't really sure if they should have any feeling about his passing. But Mike&nbsp; said he felt connected to them through Flavia and through my stories of them. I think, for him, this was a step toward corrected what his father never did for his grandkids or for Mike.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1343" _msttexthash="151462701">In 2019, he briefly married Marielena Rubio but they were annulled/divorced the same year. From accounts both from her and him directly, as was his typical, Mike was tolerable and even sometimes enjoyable from a distance, but completely intolerable to live with, and when given the ultimatum to stop drinking and start taking care of himself and start leaving the house once in a while... or leave... he chose B and left. Nobody could make Mike do anything he didn't want to do, and he didn't want to do much day-to-day unless it fed his dopamine seeking behaviors.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1344" _msttexthash="36330424">Between 2019-2023, he grew more erratic. He kept saying that any day he would go live in skid row in a tent and start using again. He never actually did, as far as I am aware. He would call every few weeks on a Saturday to check in, then sometimes he would go full incognito mode.</div><div><div><br /></div></div><div><br /></div><h4 _msthash="1345" _msttexthash="6633068" style="text-align: left;">The following are the last two major events, which I documented for Los Angeles Adult Protective Services (APS):&nbsp;</h4><div><div><ul style="text-align: left;"><li _msthash="1346" _msttexthash="5606835">7/5/23 - Mike was admitted to Los Angeles General Medical Center. Got a call from Dr Chamberlain&nbsp; (323) 409-4088</li><li _msthash="1347" _msttexthash="12228151">7/6/23 - Spoke with LA General, stated he was transferred to Kaiser Permanente Downey Medical Center, #2108. Nurse will call back with status later. 833-574-2273</li><li _msthash="1348" _msttexthash="455415051">7/6/23 - 6:15-6:30pm Kaiser Permanente Downey Medical Center. In Room# 2108. Spoke with Dr. Gunnhild Albrecht, Internal Medicine. Mike is currently in the "Step down unit (just below ICU level)". He has fluid in lungs and pneumonia. It does not appear to be COVID related. I gave her a brief medical history. His main medical concerns are fluid in lungs and low O2. He's on three anti-biotics, a diuretic, and O2 (ByPap unit). Tonight or tomorrow, they hope to have him stable enough for a CT Scan, evaluating for possible chest tube to drain fluid and determine the state of the fluid. If things get worse, they will move him into the ICU. If the ByPap and/or Chest Tube do not bring O2 into acceptable levels, they may look into progressing to intubation/ventilator while they handle the infection. He is not in good condition, but he is stable and being watched closely. He's been able to answer some questions but has mostly been sleeping since he was admitted yesterday. End Update.</li><li _msthash="1349" _msttexthash="33528651">07/14/2023 - 1:44 pm KP.org Dr. Hwang - Had been brought out of ICU, but fluid is building up again. Will need to do a new chest tube and back to ICU. Also, he was violent and agitated with the staff, had to be sedated. I gave Dr Hwang a full medical breakdown and history.&nbsp;</li><li _msthash="1350" _msttexthash="4093817">07/14/2023 - 1:50 pm **WYVERNWOOD GARDEN APARTMENTS** **2901 E Olympic Blvd Los Angeles, CA 90023**&nbsp;</li><li _msthash="1351" _msttexthash="6669988">07/20/2023 - 10:37 am Mike Wolfe texted from Hospital, thus indicating he had his cognitive capacity returned.&nbsp;</li><li _msthash="1352" _msttexthash="15722447">07/21/2023 - More text dialogues, catching up on status. Informed him that he told the other hospital he had no apartment or dog, and that his landlord called to rescue the dog.&nbsp;</li><li _msthash="1353" _msttexthash="39677092">07/24/2023 4:59 pm - Mike Wolfe added details to the events. He called 911 with a suicide attempt because he thought that would guarantee an ambulance faster than if he just called saying he couldnâ€™t breathe.&nbsp;</li><li _msthash="1354" _msttexthash="73323042">Observation: This is not a mentally sane way to view the situation or approach a failure to get oxygen. The apartment manager also added that the apartment was observed by animal rescue to be full of spider webs, cock-roaches, and was very dirty and messy. It was not a healthy situation for a human or animal, so they are not giving the dog back until they confirm it is a safe environment.</li><li _msthash="1355" _msttexthash="2961504">07/28/2023 8:20 pm Mike Wolfe texted to say he was home from the hospital.</li></ul><div><br /></div></div></div><div _msthash="1356" _msttexthash="12381291">The doctor said that he had&nbsp;Emphysema but that if he would commit to taking anti-biotics twice a day (forever? For while, never clarified) it was treatable.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1357" _msttexthash="115584586">After returning to his infested apartment, from which the dog was removed by authorities and not returned to Mike, he was eventually put in touch with a housing specialist who prevented his becoming homeless and put him into government housing at the King Edward Hotel 121 E. 5th Street Unit 218 Los Angeles, CA 90013. It was a one-room "apartment" in an old hotel, with a bathroom down the hall. There was no kitchen but a small sync in the corner. He sent this video so happy with his little set up.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><object class="BLOG_video_class" contentid="78c94155621b039d" height="266" id="BLOG_video-78c94155621b039d" width="320"></object></div><br /><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1358" _msttexthash="14257399">Early in his stay at this apartment his met his third/final "love of his life", Judith. A lovely woman, who lived across the hall from him in another similar apartment.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1359" _msttexthash="15900300">They often called together on his monthly update calls, which served him/us well in his final days, we were kept abreast of the situation, and his unfortunate deterioration.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1360" _msttexthash="7420452">On or around November 28, 2024, Mike got dizzy and fell ending with a "Massive Concussion" and was treated for a few days.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1361" _msttexthash="28066870">On Saturday, November 30, 2024, Judith and Mike called. Mike's voicemail said, "We're cancelling today" but Judith interrupted him to say that he was not in good shape and he needed to go to the hospital days ago but I'm calling the EMTs now.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1362" _msttexthash="8815313">Later that day she let me know he was taken to LA General Hospital trauma center, and once stable he would be transferred to Kaiser.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1363" _msttexthash="189532525">I also reminded her that codependency is a quick slope fall to insanity, and that while I was grateful for her taking a roll in his health, she should protect herself. He was 70, she was 82, and he would take advantage of her hospitality. She thanked me for the reminder to put her CoDA training into practice. Judith is a lovely woman whose background, heart, intellectual training, and even politics align closely with my own. I'm grateful to have known her. She was a gift to Mike and me (Darrell) in these final days. She told me that all Mike really wants these days is Netflix and to dip his vegetables into sour cream for breakfast.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1364" _msttexthash="12183353">On December 1, 2024, however, Mike left the hospital against medical advice because "there was nothing on the TV, they didn't have Netflix, and I was bored."&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1365" _msttexthash="23257234">On December 1-2, 2024, the doctor called me (as his medical point of contact) with his test results to say Mike had&nbsp;methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) and needed to be on IV Antibiotics immediately.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1366" _msttexthash="28336152">Judith confirmed he came home (he never said a word to me about it), and I told her to tell him to "stop acting like an Ass-Hat and get back there". He did not, of course. Judith and Mike did call the doctor, who sent a prescription to the pharmacy.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1367" _msttexthash="44523739">Mike refused to go back for the IV antibiotics and the doctor reluctantly send antibiotics via pill to the pharmacy, which Mike only accepted to take because Judith went with him to get them. The doctor then told Mike (with Judith on the phone) that Mike had a small brain bleed that could not be treated.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1368" _msttexthash="22050353">This began a process by which Judith attempted to get his concussion documented and get Mike officially listed as disabled (with new levels of care by the state) and potentially keep him out of a nursing home.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1369" _msttexthash="71231615">It also became apparent at this point, that he had refused to take any of the actions he was given by multiple parties to handle his bed-bug situation. He said he didn't feel them bite, so he didn't care. By December 3, 2024, there were hundreds of bed bugs, such that people couldn't enter his apartment without getting bitten. He knew what to do to handle them, but refused to do anything.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1370" _msttexthash="19651164">By December 4, 2024, Mike was refusing to bother himself with going down the hall to use the bathroom, he would pee into the sink, and sometimes have accidents from both ends on the floor by the sink.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1371" _msttexthash="103732044">He was also living almost every day with unmanaged pain from his back, which doctors were reluctant to treat with main medication due to addictive properties, but also unable to treat with any further surgeries, so he was to basically just live with the pain (2016-2025). Rarely, they would give him a cortisone shot or other treatment to mitigate the pain. But this constant pain also probably didn't bode well for his mental health or rational decision making.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1372" _msttexthash="29752294">At some point in this ordeal (possibly what led to his fall November 28), Mike had a small stroke, but again, refused treatment or diagnosis, But it became clear that this may be related to the brain bleed. This was new information after-the-fact.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1373" _msttexthash="50297299">He began stumbling around the apartment building, often confused, having to be told what to do. After many more doctor appointments in which Mike and Judith both attended, the doctor noted the memory loss and other issues, and began prepping notes that may lead to having his care moved outside of his own cognizance.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1374" _msttexthash="15302963">On December 6, he told Judith his clothes were all dirty because he had urinated in them instead of going to the bathroom. He stopped eating, and started acting more erratic.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1375" _msttexthash="6847802">On December 7, Judith reported that he was acting better, up and watching his TV. He seemed to be surviving the MRSA.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1376" _msttexthash="37844859">On December 9, Judith told me that the EMTs came again for Mike last night, and again he refused to go with them, and they couldn't force him legally until he was declared incompetent. He deteriorated quickly, hallowed in the eye, kept falling out of his char. But he refused care.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1377" _msttexthash="3897634">On December 12, Judith reported that Mike loaded up on groceries and looked better.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1378" _msttexthash="46537374">On December 13, Judith reported that Mike apparently also bought a bunch of vodka with those groceries. He refused to open the door for her, because he said the room was in terrible condition. She called EMTs and they said if he refuses to be taken, they can't do anything, but if he passess out they can take him.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1379" _msttexthash="5461599">On December 14, 2024, I (Darrell) texted Mike, "Are you still alive?", he responded on December 16, "Busy".&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1380" _msttexthash="4969731">Same day, she knocked on his door, he confirmed he was alive but refused to open the door for her.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1381" _msttexthash="74275331">On December 15, a Sunday, he came to her door demanding she ask the front office staff to do something about the bed bugs (the ones he refused to treat). She reminded him there was no staff on Sundays, he went back to bed. She let me know he's had the treatment for them since August but refused to do it. She also said you could smell how bad his room had gotten from the doorway with the door closed.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1382" _msttexthash="7676188">On December 19, Judith let me know he was making odd decisions, like announcing he was going to go out and do laundry at 2AM.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1383" _msttexthash="23862436">While it's probably due to the brain issues, this behavior sounds just like his active-addict behavior, which is a decades long pattern, so its hard to determine what do to, if anything, other than observe and report.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1384" _msttexthash="2092194">On December 21, Judith reported more of the same behaviors.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1385" _msttexthash="14053208">Christmas Day - 12/25/2024 - Mike and Judith sent a photo of him for Christmas 2024. You can see in the way he his holding his hands that he is having neurological issues.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1386" _msttexthash="35788259">On December 28, Judith and Mike returned from another round of doctor's appointments. His behavior worsened, he swears, bangs his phone down, easily frustrated. Doctor took notes of the decline. Mike made them get off the bus on the way home so he could stop and smoke.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1387" _msttexthash="9020895">I filed two reports with Los Angeles County Adult Protective services, both via website. One (869572) in July 2023 and one (1013688) on 12/06/2024.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1388" _msttexthash="158223624">On Thursday January 2, 2025, the APS worker finally showed up at Judith's door and began taking notes about the situation. That afternoon, Mike was dizzy and fell in his room again. They went to the doctor's appointment they already had scheduled, and the doctor suggested they go to the ER to get scanned. They took a bus to the ER and waited 6.5 hours and then gave up and finally left without being seen. But he was acting better than he had earlier that day, so they left. Judith's final text was they she left him in his room, with plans to do more paperwork for Mike the next day.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1389" _msttexthash="126443369">On Friday, January 3, 2025, at around 8:45 AM PST, she went to Mike's room and found him dead. EMTs were called and the LA County Coroner called me (Darrell) as next of kin to confirm the death. After a lot of phone calls with various parties, I was told that an autopsy needed to be performed, and then they would call again. I was told the apartment manager needed my permission to start cleaning up the room, which I gave. And I asked them to give anything Judith wanted to her and burn or donate the rest as appropriate.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1390" _msttexthash="3000894">Mike Wolfe died in Los Angeles California, on January 3, 2025, at 70 years old.</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEi4HmY5OsmiBXH0r4xSRM0BCBdufZpIIJYziU-95t2KOSfkJriBKScsATzIB41Is_rsi62931GqW2PiwdFHR3fmZThe6BNRLw12TAMcSWBjfvkYKiWZ9_enhcTJ0hdlb-wFesypsQh7VFyqwqZBL68B5EIYrJjtzxDVIF70BEKYAif5qkQC4U3NI1S_ocN9" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="" data-original-height="920" data-original-width="722" height="240" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEi4HmY5OsmiBXH0r4xSRM0BCBdufZpIIJYziU-95t2KOSfkJriBKScsATzIB41Is_rsi62931GqW2PiwdFHR3fmZThe6BNRLw12TAMcSWBjfvkYKiWZ9_enhcTJ0hdlb-wFesypsQh7VFyqwqZBL68B5EIYrJjtzxDVIF70BEKYAif5qkQC4U3NI1S_ocN9" width="188" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">He said this OFTEN for twenty years...&nbsp;</td></tr></tbody></table><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div></div><div><div style="text-align: left;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEiFsez8tJFoQQP_tfpXz2TRHUMq8u0hEFr7wXaV-MAtzVZuiDzDh7zA5vwSdpCrrmCqlGE-vd01H65KNB3dzFZddB9BB3hap0AkJ6suwTB-uo_tZAiEcOafSqUeyDkpyiS5HvWB0H8BI3DVNmfuZv2sMQnnHTP-6IkYZgtO-XvtR8Aalw1A5R6IiUtF4yS3" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em; text-align: center;"><img alt="" data-original-height="700" data-original-width="1184" height="189" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEiFsez8tJFoQQP_tfpXz2TRHUMq8u0hEFr7wXaV-MAtzVZuiDzDh7zA5vwSdpCrrmCqlGE-vd01H65KNB3dzFZddB9BB3hap0AkJ6suwTB-uo_tZAiEcOafSqUeyDkpyiS5HvWB0H8BI3DVNmfuZv2sMQnnHTP-6IkYZgtO-XvtR8Aalw1A5R6IiUtF4yS3" width="320" /></a></div><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div></div><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div><h4 _msthash="1391" _msttexthash="455585" style="text-align: left;">Concluding thoughts....&nbsp;</h4><div _msthash="1392" _msttexthash="1611025">Any relationship with Mike Wolfe was complicated.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><div _msthash="1393" _msttexthash="79511887">Judith described the final version of Mike (in his last year) as almost more of a happy puppy than a boyfriend (which sounds about right to me). He was of low intellect but joyful disposition (when his pain or boredom wasn't dictating his behaviors). Although he became a heavy burden at the end, he was really helpful for her, doing her dishes and helping her around her apartment for most of his last year.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div></div><div _msthash="1394" _msttexthash="20225231">Obviously, from earlier stories, his mother and sister cut him off entirely. Devin (before he died) and Ryan (to this day) want nothing much to do with Mike. But Ryan's S.O. kept in touch with Mike anyway.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1395" _msttexthash="25775139">For Debbie (his first wife and my mom) and myself (Darrell), Mike was complicated. There were things to remember with fondness, things we wished had never came to pass, and things we regretted or were even angry with him about.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1396" _msttexthash="23448750">Frankly, my dominant emotion is "relief" that my role as medical point of contact is now over. I have a few more phone calls to field and then I won't be bothered by anyone about Mike Wolfe's newest emergency ever again.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1397" _msttexthash="16148769">Yet, I find myself just a smidge sad. He was my father, and he got much worse in 1999, which means I had a few years of him being kind of OK, which Devin and Ryan really never experienced.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1398" _msttexthash="1050127">And yet, he was such a burden, many times.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1399" _msttexthash="108868838">He was impossible to have any real conversation with, because he would cut me off if what I was saying was boring him. He was only interested in what he wanted to hear, not in what I wanted to share. So, talking with him became an obligation as his oldest son, but one I was happy to offer, usually. It never led to any real connection, not with his transactional nature, but I acknowledged his attempt to be better than his father, which reminded me to be better than him for my kids.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1400" _msttexthash="25231986">In my view, Mike Wolfe was a 13-year old child who had to watch his father beat his mother, and then he had to intervene, and he really just never grew up after that. He even devolved after 1999 to a mentality similar to a 13-year old.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1401" _msttexthash="1568866">Mike loved purple, even getting purple contacts.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1402" _msttexthash="4794686">Mike Wolfe loved the band Deep Purple's "Smoke on the Water" and all of the Metalica Black album.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1403" _msttexthash="8354424">Mike loved blue-cheese burgers from Red Robin and In-n-out burgers. He often talked fondly of living near the first in-n-out.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1404" _msttexthash="10285652">Mike liked to think that he thought more deeply than he did, he made quick uninformed decisions about topics he never properly researched.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1405" _msttexthash="50451973">Having subsequently gone into biblical studies myself in my 30s, his academic conclusions were low-quality, but no lower quality than most Christian pastors I've known. The only difference between an atheist and a Calvinist is which false system they ascribe toward (but I'll save those details for my own memoirs).&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1406" _msttexthash="1717989">He loved to text with a lot of CAPITAL LETTERS and emojis.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1407" _msttexthash="1040013">He loved to say "bliss" instead of bless.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1408" _msttexthash="1635244">He often said, "You shouldn't 'should' on yourself".</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1409" _msttexthash="2380755">He often said, "Expectation is premeditated disappointment."</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1410" _msttexthash="6068127">He was quite fond of looking for simple pleasures and small joys. He loved the little dog that he lost in 2023.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1411" _msttexthash="27624974">But when I say "loved", it was almost never selfless. It was only "love" in the sense that it gave Mike some brain chemical reward. If it came between a selfless loving task that was painful and running, Mike always chose running. Always.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1412" _msttexthash="33760571">Mike Wolfe was complicated. In my own life (Darrell) Mike was never deeply negative, nor was he deeply positive, but he offered some of both. He did always demonstrate that he wanted to live Kingdom of God principles, even though he no longer believed in any God.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1413" _msttexthash="14538121">I think what I found myself having to grieve and work through in my own counseling journey, was what Mike didn't do, not what he did. He never abused us, he was just "absent".</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1414" _msttexthash="17238910">And yet, when he was in his addict state, he devolved into a very selfish nearly animal-like creature. These did cause significant inconvenience for me, usually cleaning up his mess.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1415" _msttexthash="47411065">I was praying for Mike one day, YEARS ago, close to 1999 or maybe in 1999. I got the feeling he was like&nbsp;Nebuchadnezzar in his animal state. And this really stuck with me as an image or metaphor ever since. I thought that Mike was going to either find his way back to humanity or regress further into his animal state.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1416" _msttexthash="47874827">In many ways, especially after 1999, he was more like a puppy than a man. He followed, by instinct, one pleasure to another, avoiding pain, discomfort, and boredom. His decisions took him further and further away from relationships with his fellow humans and more toward isolation and false or pseudo "bliss".&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1417" _msttexthash="12672855">Objective observers could tell he wasn't in any form of bliss, but a constant form of pain and pain avoidance... But then again, don't we all seek to avoid pain?&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1418" _msttexthash="8643752">Although, his tactics were a bit more extreme than most people I know, they were nevertheless common in the addiction circles.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1419" _msttexthash="5943561">His behavior was irrational and erratic most of the time, unless you think of him as a mammal and not a man.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1420" _msttexthash="3733912">He was at times giving and "loving" (if it served him) and at times selfish and rude.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1421" _msttexthash="18089903">In the biblical book of Daniel, chapter 4,&nbsp;Nebuchadnezzar is shown to have his reason taken due to his pride. He becomes like an animal in his demeanor and ways until he acknowledges Yahweh.</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1422" _msttexthash="82890392">I often wonder if humankind was risen out of the animal kingdom some 100,000 plus year ago and given a new form of awareness, and we then get to decide if we will join Yahweh in his kingdom and gain further awareness or regress into our mammal state and become un-human. Who knows, it's just a thought I've toyed with. (<a href="https://chatgpt.com/share/677a3933-9490-8004-a44d-1d099563c31b" target="_blank">Link to a ChatGPT</a> conversation summarizing the idea, which CS Lewis and&nbsp;<a href="https://www.premierunbelievable.com/ask-nt-wright-anything/ask-nt-wright-anything-18-hell-and-heaven/12374.article" target="_blank">NT Wright also wonders about</a>).</div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1423" _msttexthash="42282396">NT Wright says it is not for us and our theories of atonement or afterlife or 'life after life after death' to be so certain about the final state of the people who we know who have died. Wright suggests that if someone in our lives dies without an active relationship with Jesus, we should pray,&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><i _msthash="1424" _msttexthash="1520935">"Lord, I release my loved one to you and your care".&nbsp;</i></div><div><br /></div><div _msthash="1425" _msttexthash="12335713">Mike never really rejected Jesus, he simply no longer believed. I will leave his final state between Yahweh and Mike, I simply release him to Yahweh's hand.</div><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="29125967">I feel like I served him well, to his last days. I handled all my grievances in counselling years ago, and we talked about them many times years ago. We left nothing unsaid or unresolved, and agreed to disagree where we saw things differently.&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="4462822">I served Jesus by "loving my father" to the best of my ability, within healthy boundaries.&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="6902740">Of course, I also have a part of me that says, "Fuck you, Mike. Why do I have to clean up your fucking mess all the time."&nbsp;</p><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="13144196"><span _msthash="1574" _mstmutation="1" _msttexthash="30934371">Then I remind myself, I don't. I could have chosen to walk away, like many did. I chose to stay connected (within reasonable healthy boundaries), and I'm glad I did.&nbsp;</span></p><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="13144196"><span _msthash="1574" _mstmutation="1" _msttexthash="4391244"><br /></span></p><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="13144196"><span _msthash="1574" _mstmutation="1" _msttexthash="4391244"><b><u><i>This has been a written account of the life of Mike Wolfe, as told by his wordy son, Darrell.</i></u></b></span></p><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="13144196"><span _msthash="1573" _mstmutation="1" _msttexthash="1541751861"><br /></span></p><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="13144196"><span _msthash="1573" _mstmutation="1" _msttexthash="1541751861"></span></p><blockquote><p _msthash="1426" _msttexthash="13144196"><span _msthash="1573" _mstmutation="1" _msttexthash="1541751861"><i _msthash="1580" _msttexthash="1541751861">â€œAll this happened to Nebuchadnezzar the king. At the end of twelve months he was walking on the roof of the royal palace of Babylon. And the king answered and said, â€œIs this not the great Babylon which I have built as a royal palace by the strength of my own power, and for the glory of my own majesty?â€ While the words were still in the mouth of the king, a voice from heaven came, saying, â€˜To you, King Nebuchadnezzar, it is declared that the kingdom has departed from you, and you will be driven away from human society and your dwelling will be with the animals of the field and they will cause you to graze the grass like oxen,  and seven times will pass over you, until you acknowledge that the Most High is sovereign over the kingdom of humankind and that he gives it to whom he wills.â€™ Immediately the word was fulfilled concerning Nebuchadnezzar, and he was expelled from human society and he ate grass like oxen, and his body was bathed with the dew of heaven until his hair was like the hair of an eagle and his nails grew like a birdâ€™s claws. â€œBut at the end of that period, I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted up my eyes to heaven, and then my reason returned to me; and I blessed the Most High and the one who lives forever I praised and I honored. â€œFor his sovereignty is an everlasting sovereignty, and his kingdom continues from generation to generation.â€ (Daniel 4:28â€“34, LEB)</i></span></p><div></div></blockquote><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><h3 style="text-align: left;">Notes from Mike Wolfe</h3><div><br /></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEgsGvzl0Kg8uGhs6BnKwcpItcMYU6MJalt_4yULWR3mpkLpz_IcX1e1NCK5-1NxodQGe_9hWvShJpng6eD5K1rVkMHofklVXnQq_Geh9kwPCHfpDXTXCIiCWaaGGDDx0pCpKilqLu0Ddb5Zphk8e1D-bs6laGRRHEiv9TaodDz2laIj1dHZYJNt8vFrZvEo" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img alt="" data-original-height="920" data-original-width="633" height="240" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEgsGvzl0Kg8uGhs6BnKwcpItcMYU6MJalt_4yULWR3mpkLpz_IcX1e1NCK5-1NxodQGe_9hWvShJpng6eD5K1rVkMHofklVXnQq_Geh9kwPCHfpDXTXCIiCWaaGGDDx0pCpKilqLu0Ddb5Zphk8e1D-bs6laGRRHEiv9TaodDz2laIj1dHZYJNt8vFrZvEo" width="165" /></a></div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEik-8pgRG7Owm2bE3_rTs0Gp3YRxo2enf-lyLcUTN37zgAR6NxoeBYT-yBPm-MK1zKZ4UhUun6A33rHrKjLrQPP2xDoXcm7XkKT81x9VUedL3_kYu-xu2Sw4EqB4gMIN7Wf0-w5q1vUO2srKFWFcif-EO8QI8AGwklwNYznHTMXClWsIDF9n_FdT4ebOCf4" style="margin-left: 1em; font: inherit; height: 20px; margin: 4px 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span class="divider-385" color="inherit" style="border: 0px; font: inherit; height: 20px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline; width: 1px;"></span></span></div></div><div class="ms-OverflowSet-item item-178" role="none" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; display: inherit; flex-shrink: 0; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><div class="s82IJ body-157" style="-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; border: 0px; color: var(--neutralPrimary); font-family: &quot;Segoe UI&quot;, &quot;Segoe UI Web (West European)&quot;, &quot;Segoe UI&quot;, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Roboto, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-weight: inherit; line-height: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><div class="ms-TooltipHost root-169" role="none" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; display: inline; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><button aria-label="Block Dr. Mike Wolfe" class="ms-Button ms-Button--commandBar root-381" data-is-focusable="true" role="menuitem" style="-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; height: 1px; left: 0px; opacity: 0.001; position: fixed; top: 0px; width: 1px; z-index: -1;" tabindex="0"></i></div></div><div class="oNwUn IYppm" style="-webkit-box-align: baseline; -webkit-box-direction: normal; -webkit-box-orient: horizontal; -webkit-box-pack: justify; align-items: baseline; border: 0px; color: inherit; display: flex; flex-direction: row; font: inherit; justify-content: space-between; margin: 0px 0px 0px 40px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><div class="DqqDa" style="-webkit-box-flex: 1; border: 0px; color: inherit; flex: 1 1 auto; font: inherit; margin: -3px 0px 0px; min-width: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><div class="JBQMW kcAOr t9ThB" data-testid="RecipientWell" style="border: 0px; color: var(--neutralPrimary); font-family: inherit; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-size: 14px; font-stretch: inherit; font-style: inherit; font-variant: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; left: 4px; line-height: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; position: relative; vertical-align: baseline;"><div data-tabster="{&quot;mover&quot;:{&quot;cyclic&quot;:false,&quot;direction&quot;:2,&quot;memorizeCurrent&quot;:false}}" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><i aria-hidden="true" data-tabster-dummy="" role="none" style="content-visibility: hidden; height: 1px; left: 0px; opacity: 0.001; position: fixed; top: 0px; width: 1px; z-index: -1;" tabindex="0"></i><div class="___sj378o0 fly5x3f f1l02sjl f113hnb5" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; cursor: text; font: inherit; height: 19px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline; width: 746.156px;"><div aria-label="To: Darrell Wolfe &lt;me@darrellwolfe.com&gt;" aria-multiline="true" aria-readonly="true" class="VVc2u B25mw ___1rh2x4p fly5x3f f1g0x7ka fhxju0i f1qch9an f1cnd47f f1hu3pq6 f11qmguv f19f4twv f1tyq0we w_7Z_ EditorClass g7toD" role="edit" style="border: 0px; caret-color: transparent; color: inherit; cursor: auto; font-family: inherit; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-size: 12px; font-stretch: inherit; font-style: inherit; font-variant: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; font-weight: inherit; line-height: inherit; margin: 1px 0px 0px; overflow: hidden; padding: 0px; text-wrap-mode: nowrap; user-select: text; vertical-align: baseline; width: 746.156px;" tabindex="-1"><span class="_Entity _EType_LABEL_ENTITY _EReadonly_1 _GsgW B25mw" color="inherit" contenteditable="false" style="border: 0px; font: inherit; margin: 2px 3px 0px 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><label class="JRNiE WOwiK" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font-family: inherit; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-size: inherit; font-stretch: inherit; font-style: inherit; font-variant: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; height: 100%; line-height: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; user-select: none; vertical-align: baseline;">To:</label></span>â€‹<span class="_Entity _EType_RECIPIENT_ENTITY _EReadonly_1 _GsgW cOnYN B25mw" color="inherit" contenteditable="false" style="border: 0px; display: inline-block; font: inherit; margin: 2px 3px 0px 0px; max-width: calc(100% - 4px); padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span aria-haspopup="dialog" aria-label="Darrell Wolfe &lt;me@darrellwolfe.com&gt;" class="lpcWrapper lpcCommonWeb-hoverTarget container-367" color="inherit" data-is-focusable="true" data-lpc-hover-target-id="lpc-react-target-339" role="button" style="border: 0px; cursor: pointer; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;" tabindex="0"><span aria-hidden="true" class="TeT3_ V3xkQ XTLQm" color="inherit" style="-webkit-box-align: center; align-items: center; border-radius: 4px; border: 0px; box-sizing: border-box; display: inline-flex; font: inherit; margin: 0px; max-width: 100%; overflow: hidden; padding: 0px 3px 1px; position: relative; text-overflow: ellipsis; user-select: none; vertical-align: baseline;"><span class="pU1YL CzRfc jhM_9" color="var(--neutralDark)" style="border: 0px; font-family: inherit; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-size: inherit; font-stretch: inherit; font-style: inherit; font-variant: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; line-height: inherit; margin: 0px; overflow: hidden; padding: 0px; text-overflow: ellipsis; user-select: text; vertical-align: baseline;">Darrell Wolfe</span></span></span></span>â€‹</div></div><i aria-hidden="true" data-tabster-dummy="" role="none" style="content-visibility: hidden; height: 1px; left: 0px; opacity: 0.001; position: fixed; top: 0px; width: 1px; z-index: -1;" tabindex="0"></i></div></div></div><div class="srQCs" style="-webkit-box-direction: normal; -webkit-box-flex: 0; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; border: 0px; color: inherit; flex-direction: column; flex: 0 0 auto; font: inherit; margin: 0px 10px 0px 18px; min-height: 20px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><div class="AL_OM IYppm I1wdR" data-testid="SentReceivedSavedTime" style="border: 0px; color: var(--neutralPrimary); font-family: inherit; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-size: 11px; font-stretch: inherit; font-style: inherit; font-variant: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; line-height: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; text-align: right; vertical-align: baseline;">Sat 2/13/2016 7:54 AM</div></div></div></div><div class="IYppm T3idP hg7Eg" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px 16px 0px 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: top;"></div><div role="document" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><div aria-label="Message body" class="ulb23 GNqVo allowTextSelection OuGoX" id="UniqueMessageBody_150" style="border: 0px; color: var(--neutralDark); cursor: auto; font-family: inherit; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-size: 15px; font-stretch: inherit; font-style: inherit; font-variant: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; line-height: inherit; margin: 16px 0px 10px; outline: none 0px; overflow-y: auto; padding: 0px 0px 2px; user-select: text; vertical-align: baseline; will-change: scroll-position;" tabindex="0"><div style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;" visibility="visible"><div class="rps_be10" style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><div style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;">I'm 61-y.o &amp; my eldest son is now 35. &nbsp;When sensing the author's same sense he needed to spend more quality/quantity of time w/ his boys, I chose to enroll my eldest (then 12) &amp; I in Group Golf lessons at the local Community College. &nbsp;I learned 5-10 yrs. later I had been self-centered &amp; clueless even while attempting to meet his needs for a dad who cared deeply about him. &nbsp;My 12-y.o. sat squatted on the driving range much of the lesson-time, picking grass blades during boredom periods, I think frustrated w/ his progress &amp; and disintressed in the whole experience. &nbsp;This author has apparently hit-gold, having dug staight into the Mother-Load vein of priceless gold of Other-Centered, Joyous service to his boys. &nbsp; I am INTENSELY proud of the author's insight &amp; choice! &nbsp;<div style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><br /></div><div style="border: 0px; color: inherit; font: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span face="&quot;Segoe UI&quot;, &quot;Segoe UI Web (West European)&quot;, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Roboto, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif" style="background-color: #292929; color: white;">P. S. &nbsp;He is that 12-y.o. of yesteryear.&nbsp;</span><br style="background-color: #292929; color: white; font-family: &quot;Segoe UI&quot;, &quot;Segoe UI Web (West European)&quot;, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Roboto, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif;" /><div style="background-color: #292929; border: 0px; color: white; font-family: &quot;Segoe UI&quot;, &quot;Segoe UI Web (West European)&quot;, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Roboto, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-stretch: inherit; font-variant-alternates: inherit; font-variant-east-asian: inherit; font-variant-emoji: inherit; font-variant-numeric: inherit; font-variant-position: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; line-height: inherit; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><br /><br /><div><br /></div><h4 style="text-align: left;">11/26/2015</h4><div><br /></div><div>Dr. Mike Wolfe&lt;mikewolfesouldoc@yahoo.com&gt;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Debbie Tweedy;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Darrell Wolfe;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Flavia Wolfe;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Ryan Wolfe</div><div>â€‹</div><div>As I flourish here in my Plush Recliner, EVERYthing I need OR even WANT, w/in&nbsp; my "Grip 'n Grab's" EZ reach, Fridge jammed so full tis hard to keep door closed, WIDE variety of feasting, snacking, relishing, savoring culinary delights BEGGING to be first down my gullet, car paid off in perfect condition &amp; gas tank filled to brim, surgery for brand new hip healing amazingly qic &amp; thorough, EACH of Uuuuuuuuu in varrying depths of intimate communication w/ my deepest soul/spirit/Self, making more $ than I ever thought possible (and relatively SECURELY so), 3-INCREDIBLE Grandkids who greatly enhance my sense of purpose &amp; daily joy, more TV Channels than hairs on mi cabeza (with DIRECT &amp; FULL access to "ALL-Things-Sports,including HOCKEY (a sport to which Ryan enticed me, schooled me, and during which live coverage of Stanley-Cup-Playoffs, I'd actually/playfully curse, AKA, "F-in'-A Ryan, d Kings did their magic again!"),&nbsp; a comfty-WARM-Silk-Sheeted bed awaiting my every whim to slide in over &amp; snuggle thru a luxurious nap, 36" from a door that opens to reveal an ASTOUNDING mountain landscape of valleys-hills-meadows---&amp; a small, uh, TINY town ambiance of community (and where all the servers &amp; owners know my name &amp; bring my "to-order" preferences straight to my car, with 3.25 wks. of sheer/pure Vaca followed by a career/service-opportunity gets me up energized to re-connect w/ my colleagues/Probation-compadres/leaders/supervisors daily share how much I enhance their own service-sense, miss my presence &amp; seek my quick return,&nbsp; with a "Preferred-Purposes-STATUS" just 6-yrs./8-mos. hence, wherein my life is Serve-Others at a pace I choose, concomitant w/ sheer hedonistic pleasure-reward-fun/frolic,</div><div><br /></div><div>and MOST of all THIS Thanksgiving:</div><div><br /></div><div>With a deep, abiding, rational/emotive conviction, that w/ all my challenges thru life, foibles, narcissism, control-freak, tunnel-visioned;&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>I possess that GLORIOUS experience of "knowing" at my soul's deepest reaches that I am SMACK-dab in the direct middle of what the Universe's CALL for me IS ! ! !&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>En-JOY the Holiday which alone highlights/reinforces the widely verified truth:&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The SINGLE-most correlate w/ Happiness/JOY, is GRATITUDE/Thanksgiving !&nbsp; &nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>O!, and now would u be so kind to get ur freakin' shovel out from under that LAST helping of Bread-Dressing-Smothered-In-Turkey-Gravy, and pass me a bit!&nbsp; &nbsp; lol</div><div><br /></div><div>Love,</div><div>Dad/Mike/Grandpa/Papa-Mike</div><div><br /></div><br /><br /><br /><h4 style="text-align: left;">11/09/2015</h4><br /><div>Dr. Mike Wolfe&lt;mikewolfesouldoc@yahoo.com&gt;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Debbie Tweedy;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Darrell Wolfe;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Flavia Wolfe;</div><div>â€‹</div><div>Ryan Wolfe</div><div>â€‹</div><div><br /></div><div>Inline image BARING some unforeseen delay (&amp; know they fo not CONFIRM 100% Ur scheduled date/time will HOLD til the AFTERNOON b4), I get to Hosp. @ 8a Thurs. for 10:30a Actual Surg. (pic is the Blood Type/Screen done 9a today &amp; CANNOT remove).&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Good news released by Pre-Op RN (btw Debster, I generally say, "I was married to sn RN for 20-yrs, so I know some of what ur service means".&nbsp; [I am PROUD of U Deb, &amp; ALL of WHO u ARE],&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>anyway, RN revealed I am Dr. Hopkin's #2-Surg. (OF Freakin' 6) Thurs AM-PM!&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div>He works 3 of his 4 days off to accomodate clts, he is SUCH demand [&amp; quite likely ADD to his retirement funds, as he is nearing same by appearance] lol</div><div><br /></div><div>I figure: #1 will warm him up, &amp; I'll get him b4 he gets tired!&nbsp; lol&nbsp; lol&nbsp; lol</div><div><br /></div><div>My Strong-LOVE to each of U!!!</div><div><br /></div><div>Mike/Dad/Grandpa/Papa-Mike&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><h4 style="text-align: left;">10/05/2015</h4><br /><div>Pre-Op appts over next few wks. &amp; UNLESS they have a cancelation by someone scheduled EARLIER, my hard-date for the Total Rt. Hip Replacement surgery is Thurs. 11/12/15 (one does not know the time til a few days b4. Be in Hosp. overnite &amp; (unless there are complications), I go home to recuperate. Back2Work usually is MD approved in 4-6 wks.</div><div><br /></div><div>Thanks for asking. :)</div><div><br /></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></div>
